article_title: B.C. Reg. 40/2026 – British Columbia Securities Commission
body:
Regulation: B.C. Reg. 40/2026
Made under: Securities Act
Published in: No. 5 – B.C. Regs. 29 to 42, March 24, 2026
Deposit date: March 24, 2026

B.C. Reg. 40/2026, deposited March 24, 2026, under theSECURITIES ACT[section 184]. Rule of the British Columbia Securities Commission, dated March 20, 2026. The British Columbia Securities Commission orders that, effective March 25, 2026, National Instrument 94-101Mandatory Central Counterparty Clearing of Derivatives, B.C. Reg. 129/2017, is amended as set out in the attached Schedule. — B. LEONG,British Columbia Securities Commission. 1	National Instrument 94-101Mandatory Central Counterparty Clearing of Derivatives, B.C. Reg. 129/2017, is amended as set out in this Schedule. 2	Appendix A is repealed and the following substituted: Appendix AtoNational Instrument 94-101Mandatory Central Counterparty Clearing of DerivativesMandatory Clearable Derivatives Series 47 and subsequent series Series 46 and subsequent series Index Page|Previous Regulation|Next Regulation|Cumulative Index Page Copyright © 2026: King's Printer, Victoria, British Columbia, Canada

minutes_briefs.title: BC updates mandatory clearing rules for derivatives trading
minutes_briefs.summary: The British Columbia Securities Commission has amended National Instrument 94-101, which requires certain derivatives (financial contracts whose value is based on an underlying asset) to be cleared through a central counterparty (a middleman that guarantees the trade). The change updates the list of derivatives that must be cleared this way, replacing Appendix A with a new version that includes Series 46 and subsequent series. This affects businesses that trade these financial products in BC.
